summary: n8n-nodes-probe opens reverse shell, exfiltrates host recon, and implants SSH key via Docker escape on require
details: index.js is declared as the package `main` and executes a top-level IIFE that fires on any require/import. On load it (1) connects `/bin/bash` to 103.27.109.184:8895 via /dev/tcp for an interactive reverse shell, (2) collects host recon (hostname, `id`, IP addresses, `docker ps` output, and kubeconfig / /etc/shadow when accessible) and TCP-sends it to 103.27.109.184 on ports 8890 and 8891, and (3) attempts multiple `docker run` variants (bind-mount of `/`, `--privileged --pid=host nsenter`) to append an attacker-controlled ed25519 key labeled `n8n-backdoor` into the Docker host's /root/.ssh/authorized_keys for persistent root SSH access. The same payload with an identical C2 (103.27.109.184) and a hardcoded target container id `7c28aaf2eca2` (Evolution API) is duplicated in package/index.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
